findings.reachability: Reachable by any caller of encryptKeyInfo/encryptKeyInfoWithScheme that passes a non-null options.pem value which does not contain a valid PEM certificate header/footer. The truthiness check at line 46/76 of xmlenc.js prevents null/undefined, but any non-empty non-PEM string bypasses it.
findings.justification: RegExp.prototype.exec() returns null when there is no match; the subsequent cert.length access on null throws TypeError: Cannot read properties of null. The enclosing try/catch in xmlenc.js encryptKeyInfoWithScheme (lines 19-38) catches it and delivers it to the callback, preventing a process crash, but it makes the encrypt operation fail on any malformed certificate.

detection_pattern.section_name: Null Deref: regex exec() result unchecked
detection_pattern.description: Detects patterns where the return value of RegExp.exec() is used for a property access (e.g. .length, [1]) without a null check. exec() returns null on no match, so any property access will throw TypeError.
